PAGEREF _Toc162346855 \h 11 13.3 Acknowledgement PAGEREF _Toc162346856 \h 11 Enhancement Revision History Changed By Date Version Notes: Cody Ramangkoun 03 / 1 4 /202 4 0.0 Original document EX 94 – Allocate Cigarettes by Order Line Priority McLane sells cigarettes which require jurisdiction specific stamping before shipping. The stamping is either pre-stamping ( during the replenishment to the pick location ) or post-stamping ( after the inventory is picked for an order ) . During the replenishment of these cigarettes the unstamped inventory is used to replenish a jurisdiction specific pick location. Cigarette Fast and Slow mover are interfaced with a specific allocation type field (Wave Allocation Type - sys code B-325), which takes precedence over other allocation sequencing. Cigarette order lines in the o rder are interfaced with specific allocation type to be allocated from specific jurisdiction. Allocation priorities for cigarette active locations are setup such that all locations for one jurisdiction are associated to a pick determinization zone . During allocation for cigarette items there may not be sufficient inventory to fulfill order needs from the active pick location for a given jurisdiction. WMOS leverages EX01 and EX11 to extend custom cigarette item replenishment logic and Active -to-Active cigarette item r eplenishment , respectively . EX94 is utilized to enhance the allocation priority of Cigarettes based on ORDER_LINE_ITEM . PRIORITY throughout the base waving proces s . The ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRIORITY will be used in order selection during the wave process to ensure the highest priority – lowest number lines are fulfilled first. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y Reporting N Other N Wave Allocation and Replenishment to Support Cigarette Inventory Conversion Assumptions Locations and process mentioned in this document are only for cigarettes management area. All other items will u tilize base allocation logic . EX94 is utilized during the Cigarette Replenishment Wave (Cigarette Forecast Replenishment) only . Each pre-stamped cigarette jurisdiction has dedicated active pick locations. In WMOS, this is determined by configuring pick determinization zone (PDZ) per jurisdiction and assigning PDZ to corresponding locations. ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRIORITY m ust be a positive integer in the range 1 - 999. Picking for orders is executed only in jurisdiction specific active locations (INT50), unstamped (post-stamped) active locations (INT50) and bulk case pick locations (INT51).     Unstamped active locations are common active location where  oLPNs  for post-stamp jurisdiction are allocated.    Order lines for cigarettes have allocation type populated. Allocation type in order line is corresponding to stamp jurisdiction.    Allocation Priority for custom INT-99 is configured with proper unstamped PDZ as allocation source . An item can either be a cigarette, utilizing EX01 logic, or can have item substitutions configured, utilizing EX05.  An item cannot be setup as a cigarette and have item substitutions     Standard allocation functions are supported for custom INT. Active to Active replenishment INT is only generated through the waving process. Inventory moves from a jurisdiction specific location to another jurisdiction location must be manual process and is out of scope of this extension. Soft allocation is out of scope for this extension. If unable to allocate through fast-moving allocation (EX01), cigarettes will attempt to allocate through slow-moving allocation (EX11). Slow-moving allocation types and fast-moving allocation types share the same three letter code. The fast-moving allocation type is entered as all capital letters, e.g., ‘AFQ’, and the slow-moving allocation type is stored as ‘ afq ’. Dependencies Code Dependencies EX01 – Cigarettes Inventory Conversion EX11 – Cigarette Active to Active Replenishment EX05 and EX11 extensions will be used in same wave but against different items. Wave and allocation processing should support both. Interface Dependencies Interface : Field Requirement : Distribution Order LineItem -> Priority Priority for Inventory Allocation P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Change/Addition Wave Allocation See section 7.1 Wave Replenishment Allocation in the MCLN EX 01 Cigarettes Inventory Conversion extension specification document. Custom Cigarette Inventory replenishment allocations/tasks generated through EX 94 are expected to follow the EX 01 inventory conversion execution and exception processes. See section 7.1 Custom Active - to - Active Replenishment INT Generation in the MCLN EX 1 1 Cigarettes Active to Active Replenishment extension specification document. Custom Active to Active replenishment allocations/tasks generated through EX 94 are expected to follow the EX 1 1 inventory conversion execution and exception processes. Additionally, WMOS is customized to enhance the allocation priority of cigarette picking tasks and Cigarette Replenishments based on ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRIORITY . Process Steps : WMOS wave allocation identifies opportunities for replenishment per base requirements . Before any custom replenishment requirements, WMOS first considers base replenishment opportunities for the same item need. All allocation priorities for INT 3 are considered before moving into custom replenishment. If base cannot replenish the same item, WMOS custom logic identifies if the item has available inventory for conversion. If available, WMOS attempts to allocate the inventory for replenishing the item need following the same priorities setup for INT 3. All priorities are considered for the inventory conversion item before continuing to the next cigarette inventory conversion. IF there is limited inventory for cigarette conversion , WMOS will leverage ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRIORITY to allocate INT50 cigarette picking tasks and INT3 Replenishments based on highest priority - lowest value. WMOS checks if allocation type is flagged for custom less than case allocation else proceeds with base replenishment . IF allocation type is flagged for less than case allocation , WMOS checks if item demand can be met from unstamped active location and creates INT99 Active to Active replenishment . IF there is limited unstamped cigarette inventory, WMOS will leverage ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRIORITY to allocate INT50 cigarette picking t asks and INT99 Active to Active Replenishments based on highest priority - lowest value . ELSE IF inventory is insufficient to fulfill demand from unstamped active location, WMOS invokes EX11 custom updates to generate additional INT3 processing to over allocate to a full standard pack quantit y to the unstamped active location OR Reserve using base INT9 Replenish Case Pick from reserve allocation . If the custom replenishment for inventory conversion fails, WMOS will make base shortage updates. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Then , the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 94 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 94 is turned off, and no custom EX 94 processing will occur. Last, the wave-specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the Picking Wave Parameters Processing Attributes, Attribute 2 . This field is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX94 is turned off for the wave, and no custom EX 94 processing will occur   Validations WMOS is enhanced to do the following custom validation. WM validates the custom system level configuration is enabled for EX94. WM validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX01 custom cigarette item replenishment logic . IF EX01 is enabled for custom cigarette item replenishment logic , continue as per custom processing. ELSE, no custom cigarette item replenishment t logic is invoked, and we continue as per base processing. WM validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX11 custom Active-to-Active replenishment logic. IF EX11 Active-to-Active replenishment logic invoked, continue as per custom processing. ELSE, no Active-Active replenishment logic is invoked, and we continue as per base processing. WM validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the Picking Wave Parameters Processing Attributes, Attribute 2 . IF the Picking Wave Parameters Processing Attributes, Attribute 2 is set as ‘Enable EX94’ , continue as per custom processing. ELSE, we continue as per base processing. WM validates that the ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRIORITY is populated IF populated, continue as per custom updates . ELSE, ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRIORITY IN (‘NULL’, ‘0’) and we do not perform custom updates called out in EX94. Updates WMOS performs the waving process for cigarette inventory updates per EX01 and EX11 . In addition to these updates, WMOS creates custom allocation updates based on ORDER_LINE_ITEM . PRIORITY in ascending order. If ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRIORITY are equal, then WMOS will leverage base a llocation priority , rules, and parameters for cigarette picking task and cigarette inventory replenishment generation logic within EX94 . Undo Wave See section 7.1.5 Undo Wave in the MCLN EX 1 1 Cigarettes Active to Active Replenishment extension specification document. Custom Active to Active replenishment allocations/tasks generated through EX 94 are expected to follow the EX 0 1 inventory conversion execution and exception processes. During the undo wave, the custom changes made for the INT-3 Replenish Active from Case Pick and INT-99 Replenish Active from Active need to be undone. As EX11 Note: as part of the replenishment generation in this section which creates the allocation, WMOS creates a custom dummy Pick Location Detail for the item with a NULL item attribute 1. This is to allow for appropriate allocation and task references. This custom created dummy PLD is only transient while the allocation/task exists. When the task or allocation is completed, cancelled, or shorted, the updates occur to the original and permanent Pick Location Detail with defined Item Attribute 1 value. The dummy PLD is decremented and ultimately deleted in that process. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. Process Change/Addition No Process changes/additions in scope for this extension Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label, or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front . Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. If the question resulted in any action it is listed against the question. # Asked By Question Answered By Answer Date Added Action Taken 1 2 3 4 Estimates and Acknowledgement All services provided are pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Acknowledgement for Consulting Services or the Software Services Agreement previously entered into between our respective companies and these Agreements provide that such services are billed at the hourly billing rates in effect at the time services are rendered. This acknowledgement is to c over the estimate given below. Please note that the estimate below is good for only 60 days from the date the quote is provided. Description of Requested Project The below estimate covers time required for design, development, unit testing, and client issue support related to this enhancement. Estimated Cost Solution: Estimated $ WMS TBD SCI LM EEM SO Total: Acknowledgement Your signature is required as proof of acceptance of the extension described above and the estimated cost in order for programming to begin .
